vc035_artifact_analysis.claim: VC035 is a detector coordinate-threshold artifact of removing Panel A's leftward protrusion, not a new or worsened real defect.
evidence: Panel D source (lines 139-165, scope shift (0,0)) is byte-identical between B and C; only line 25 (Panel A) changed. TikZ renders each scope from its own byte-identical source, so Panel D internal clearances are mathematically unchanged.

Removing the -0.262cm protrusion made the page 0.367cm (86.6px @600dpi) narrower, shifting all absolute pixel coordinates; the visual_clash detector uses absolute pixels, so a sub-threshold near_miss on '$I(t)$' near its own meter box crossed the reporting floor.

Pixel comparison of the Panel D 'I(t)' region after 87px re-alignment: 96.7% byte-identical (33091/34226 px), residual 3.3% is low-magnitude (max intensity 93, never a full 255 flip) edge antialiasing from the 0.6px sub-pixel offset residual.
VC035 promotion_tier is null (not queued) and source_attribution is null; it carries no actionable weight.
vc035_artifact_analysis.conclusion: No mandatory stop: repair introduces no real new collision and worsens no confirmed defect (docs/figure-agent.md section 6).

regression_verdict: PASS (machine): no new/worsened real defect; the one new near_miss is a sub-pixel coordinate artifact; the target defect (frame protrusion) is removed.
